title: hieracium septentrionale subsp. tenebricans
intro: Hieracium septentrionale subsp. tenebricans, also known as the lesser hawk's beard, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and skin irritations. The plant is rich in flavonoids and saponins, which are believed to have antioxidant and antiseptic effects, providing various health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Hieracium septentrionale subsp. tenebricans are often dried and used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
